description_long: This object is a long, slender broom with a natural wood appearance, featuring a straight cylindrical handle that extends upward and splits into two angled branches near the top. The handle is smooth and unadorned, with a consistent wood grain texture visible along its length. The branching at the top suggests a rustic or handmade design, possibly intended for outdoor or utility use. There are no visible bristles or sweeping elements attached, making it resemble a traditional wooden broomstick or a tool for hanging or supporting other items. The surface finish appears matte, with no glossy or reflective qualities. No brand markings, labels, or additional attachments are visible on any side.
description: A long, wooden broomstick with a smooth cylindrical handle and two angled branches at the top, featuring a natural wood grain and a matte finish.
description_view_0: A straight-on view of the broom's handle, showing its slim cylindrical profile with no visible branches or attachments.
description_view_1: This view reveals the two angled branches at the top of the handle, highlighting the broom's forked design.
description_view_2: A direct side view of the handle, similar to view 0, with the branches not visible from this angle.
description_view_3: The angled branches at the top are again visible, mirroring the perspective of view 1.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with a round cross-section, smooth surface, and no visible grip enhancements or end caps.
attribute_bristle_arrangement: No bristles are visible; the top of the broom features two angled wooden branches instead of a traditional bristle arrangement.
attribute_mounting_position: Intended to be leaned vertically against a surface; no visible holes, hooks, or mounting features.
